fact: October 4 is exactly 277 days into the year, which means there are only 88 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun is setting about 2 minutes earlier each day around October 4, making the days shrink faster than a deflating balloon.
Maple trees are pumping out their brightest red and orange colors right now because they're basically having a sugar rush - all that stored sugar creates the brilliant colors!
October 4 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, which means the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were almost equal length just two weeks ago!
Monarch butterflies are flying over 2,000 miles south right now, and the crazy part is that the butterflies making this journey have never been to their destination before!
The moon phase around October 4 changes every year, but when it's a new moon on this date, you can see about 2,000 more stars than usual because there's no moonlight washing them out.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 4 is prime baby animal season - kangaroo joeys are peeking out of pouches and penguin chicks are getting fluffy!
Morning frost starts appearing on grass around this time in colder places, creating millions of tiny ice crystals that look like nature's glitter bomb exploded overnight.
October 4 is roughly 13 days after the autumn equinox, which means we're officially in the 'getting darker fast' part of fall in the north.
Spiders are at their absolute biggest and fattest right now because they've been eating bugs all summer long - some orb weavers are three times heavier than they were in spring!
The air pressure tends to drop around early October, which is why some people's grandparents claim their joints can predict the weather - they actually might be onto something!
